title: Blisters
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives.
supports: Blisters are fluid-filled sacs on the outer layer of skin.
Friction, burns, infection, and some diseases can cause blisters.
Simple friction blisters can often be protected while they heal.
Infection, burns, widespread blistering, drug-rash concern, diabetic foot risk, or immunocompromise require separate clinician-directed care.
